purpose: Map the Ascend TorchTitan-NPU/CANN AutoFuse DeepSeek-V4-Flash performance recipe items to the active AMD MI350 DeepSeek-V4-style pretraining benchmark work.

notable_lines.headline_gains: lines 1-16: pure FSDP plus large EP, AutoFuse vector fusion, sparse attention fused operators, 1100 tokens/p/s on 64 A3 cards.
notable_lines.parallel_shape: lines 40-44: TP=1, PP=1, VPP=1, EP=128, FSDP=128, MBS=1, GBS=1024.
notable_lines.fsdp_memory_and_overlap: lines 107-141: FSDP memory relief, compute-hidden communication, and separate FSDP/EP process groups.
notable_lines.optimizer_offload: lines 143-149: SwapOptimizer reduces AdamW resident memory.
notable_lines.autofuse: lines 151-225: torch.compile plus AutoFuse vector fusion and host-bound reduction.
notable_lines.custom_attention_ops: lines 231-359: LightningIndexer, SparseAttnSharedkv, SparseAttnSharedkvGrad, and SparseLightningIndexerGradKLLoss.
notable_lines.perf_breakdown: lines 363-406: throughput from 397 to 1100 tokens/p/s, custom ops about 90 percent gain, AutoFuse about 30 percent gain, compute 84.68 percent, EP plus FSDP 12.53 percent, MFU 28.78 percent.

execution_policy.selected_path: direct TorchTitan torchrun with a real DeepSeek-V4 config
execution_policy.proxy_only: DeepSeek-V3 16B evidence is retained for systems plumbing only and must not be promoted as DeepSeek-V4.

eight_mi350.shape: TP=1 PP=1 CP=1 EP=8 FSDP=8 MBS=1 S=4096 GBS=128
eight_mi350.source: README.md and recipe.yaml active target basis

mapping.ascend_role: TorchTitan-NPU adapts TorchTitan to CANN communication and operator acceleration while preserving a TorchTitan-shaped training flow.
mapping.amd_evidence: sources/targets/torchtitan_deepseek_v4_flash_4layer_bf16_4x8xmi350_canary.yaml defines the real DeepSeek-V4 target contract, and launchers/torchtitan_direct_deepseek_v4_4x8xmi350_canary.sh uses direct torchrun while refusing to substitute DeepSeek-V3 16B. The old Primus path remains proxy-only in run_artifacts/torchtitan_canary_runtime.yaml.
mapping.gap_or_action: Materialize torchtitan.models.deepseek_v4 and a DeepSeek-V4 Flash 4-layer registry/config in the selected TorchTitan checkout, then run the direct launcher.

mapping.ascend_item: Pure FSDP plus large EP with no TP or PP
mapping.ascend_role: Avoids TP/PP complexity and reduces memory pressure with EP128 and FSDP128.
mapping.amd_evidence: AMD target is TP=1 PP=1 CP=1 EP=4/8 FSDP=4/8 MBS=1 S=4096 GBS=64/128. The new target contract records DeepSeek-V4-Flash 4-layer model identity and the DeepSeek-V3 16B proxy is labeled proxy-only.
mapping.gap_or_action: Still need the real TorchTitan DeepSeek-V4 model implementation and a direct 4x/8x training run.

mapping.ascend_item: Avoid TP for DeepSeek-V4 attention/indexer semantics
mapping.ascend_role: Indexer/head reductions make TP costly or correctness-sensitive because extra AllGather would be needed.
mapping.amd_evidence: The experiment goal and configs defer TP: tensor_parallel_degree=1 for the 8x target.
mapping.gap_or_action: Full DSv4 indexer and sparse-attention TorchTitan integration remains missing; TP deferral avoids this for first canary only.

mapping.ascend_role: Embedding, LmHead, MTP, and MoE load imbalance make PP hard to tune.
mapping.gap_or_action: No immediate PP work; revisit only if memory forces PP after FSDP/EP and activation policy are exhausted.

mapping.ascend_item: FSDP memory relief for non-expert parameters
mapping.ascend_role: After EP sharding, non-expert parameters become the memory bottleneck; FSDP shards parameters, gradients, and optimizer state.
mapping.amd_evidence: DeepSeek-V3 16B proxy canary reached about 40.55 GiB rank-0 memory at GBS128 checkpoint-free clean steps.
mapping.gap_or_action: Need real 43-layer Flash model memory audit, including optimizer state, activation checkpointing, and checkpoint save/resume size.

mapping.ascend_role: A3 SuperPod bandwidth lets FSDP AllGather and ReduceScatter overlap with model compute.
mapping.amd_evidence: The AMD proxy has throughput logs but no RCCL stream-level trace proving FSDP AG/RS is hidden.
mapping.gap_or_action: Run a short 8x rocprof/RCCL trace once do-sonle5-mi350-gpu is idle, with FSDP AG/RS, EP AllToAll, and compute ranges separated.

mapping.ascend_item: Separate FSDP and EP communication domains
mapping.ascend_role: Ascend found shared ProcessGroup serialized FSDP and EP communication; splitting domains reduced MoE communication wait from about 10 ms to about 3 ms and improved training throughput.
mapping.amd_evidence: The unpatched 4x runtime probe in run_artifacts/torchtitan_fsdp_ep_pg_probe_4x_20260531.json reports fsdp_group_is_ep_group=true on every rank. The patched 4x probe in run_artifacts/torchtitan_fsdp_ep_pg_probe_4x_dedicated_ep_20260531.json reports fsdp_group_is_ep_group=false on every rank and passes FSDP all_reduce plus EP all_to_all smoke.
mapping.gap_or_action: Carry the EP DeviceMesh ProcessGroup isolation into the selected direct TorchTitan runtime, rerun 4x/8x pg-probe, then run a short RCCL/rocprof overlap trace.

mapping.ascend_role: Reduces resident AdamW optimizer state memory by offloading moments to host and pipelining load-update-unload.
mapping.amd_evidence: Current AMD milestone intentionally uses AdamW for bring-up; Muon and optimizer offload are future work.
mapping.gap_or_action: Defer until real DSv4-Flash memory shows optimizer state pressure; keep checkpointable AdamW stable first.

mapping.ascend_role: AutoFuse produces about 31.8 percent end-to-end gain by fusing vector ops and reducing host-bound gaps.
mapping.amd_evidence: The AMD canary launcher forces --compile.enable false. Single-layer work has hand/prototype Triton and AITER kernels but no broad graph compiler fusion pass.
mapping.gap_or_action: First profile vector and launch-bound time on AMD. Then evaluate TorchInductor/Triton/CK/AITER fusion selectively instead of assuming AutoFuse maps directly.

mapping.ascend_role: Compiled/fused execution reduces host dispatcher and launch bubbles between heavyweight matmuls.
mapping.amd_evidence: Single-layer rocprof artifacts exist, but the 8x proxy runtime has no full host-bound/launch attribution.
mapping.gap_or_action: Add 8x profiler run with trace kept outside repo and summarize FREE/host-bound equivalent in run_artifacts.

mapping.ascend_role: Computes top-k indexer path with pipelined cube/vector phases.
mapping.amd_evidence: Current AMD single-layer sparse MLA work covers attention-style kernels; production TorchTitan DSv4 LightningIndexer is not integrated.
mapping.gap_or_action: Use NVIDIA/Ascend references to define LI forward/backward contracts before final DSv4 model bring-up.

mapping.ascend_role: Fuses gather, QK, online softmax with sink, PV, and rescale for sparse/shared-KV attention.
mapping.amd_evidence: Flash S4096 sparse MLA forward is about 4.02 ms versus 3.6 ms target in run_artifacts/single_layer_target_correction_20260531.json.
mapping.gap_or_action: Continue sparse attention forward specialization only after backward/whole-layer priorities; current gap is about 1.12x.

mapping.ascend_role: Fuses backward gather/cube/process/scatter with preload and CV pipeline.
mapping.amd_evidence: Split-compressed sparse MLA backward improved median from 5.07 ms to 4.60 ms with exact checks; CSA, split softmax, and sparse window families remain the largest local primitives.
mapping.gap_or_action: Next kernel focus: CSA compressed-score/grad/value-add sequence, split softmax backward, and sparse window q/selected accumulation.

mapping.ascend_role: Fuses main attention score, LI backward, and KL loss to avoid sequence-squared memory.
mapping.amd_evidence: The first AMD canary is a synthetic/small-token training-system path; LI auxiliary/KL training is not yet in scope.
mapping.gap_or_action: Mark as required only once the full DSv4 training objective includes LI auxiliary/indexer loss.

mapping.ascend_role: Ascend future work calls out avoiding redundant recompute of vector ops that do not need saved activations.
mapping.gap_or_action: Profile activation recompute cost and introduce selective policy after correctness and group/comm tracing are stable.

mapping.ascend_item: EP communication/compute overlap or MC2-style scheduling
mapping.ascend_role: Ascend lists this as future work; EP communication remains a named optimization frontier.
mapping.amd_evidence: TorchTitan token dispatcher uses all_to_all_single_autograd for EP dispatch/combine. Primus/Megatron references include DeepEP-related controls, but the current TorchTitan canary uses standard EP AllToAll.
mapping.gap_or_action: Trace EP AllToAll exposure first; then decide whether ROCm path needs RCCL stream scheduling, fused dispatch/compute, or a ROCm DeepEP-equivalent.

mapping.ascend_role: Ascend lists Muon as future convergence work, not a prerequisite for reported perf.
mapping.amd_evidence: AMD mixed Muon/AdamW smoke/preflight artifacts exist, but current milestone remains AdamW.
mapping.gap_or_action: Do not block the first DSv4-style systems canary on Muon.

evidence: lines 127-136: dense and sparse meshes are created by separate unflatten operations; comment says DeviceMesh currently recreates process groups for each dimension.
lines 167-203: for TP=1 CP=1 PP=1 EP=8 FSDP=8 world_size=8, fsdp=8 and efsdp=1, and the sparse mesh has ep=8.
lines 213-226: ep comes from full_sparse_mesh['ep']; fsdp comes from full_dense_mesh['fsdp'].
parallel_dims.read: Static local source suggests FSDP and EP can be distinct meshes even when rank sets match, but this must be verified in the active runtime because DeviceMesh/process-group behavior is backend and version sensitive.

evidence: lines 218-230: GroupedExperts wires ep_mesh into the token dispatcher.
lines 276-310: dispatch uses ep_mesh for all_to_all_single and all_to_all_single_autograd.
combine uses all_to_all_single_autograd with the same ep_mesh.
ep_dispatch.read: EP traffic is tied to the DeviceMesh object passed from ParallelDims, so the runtime group identity of ep_mesh is the thing to inspect.

evidence: lines 75-100: FSDP uses fsdp mesh for dense params and optional efsdp mesh for EP regions, then calls apply_fsdp with ep_degree and edp_mesh.
fsdp_application.read: For the 8x EP8/FSDP8 target, efsdp is size 1, so expert-region FSDP is mostly mixed-precision wrapping rather than a second sharding axis; dense FSDP is the main all-gather/reduce-scatter domain.

evidence: ep_process_group_pass.py lines 7-15: explicitly scans EP all-to-alls and rewrites them to an extra process group if they share a process-group object with non-EP collectives.
ep_process_group_pass.py lines 140-165: creates and rewrites EP process groups only when object-identical sharing is detected.
fsdp_passes.py lines 61-64 and 110-126: creates extra FSDP all-gather process groups to put all-gathers and reduce-scatters on different streams.
graph_trainer_passes.read: This is a source-level match to the Ascend process-group concern, but it belongs to graph_trainer/compile passes.

evidence: run_artifacts/torchtitan_canary_runtime.yaml records the effective runtime as image-bundled Primus/TorchTitan, not the local WIP snapshot.
run_artifacts/torchtitan_fsdp_ep_pg_probe_4x_20260531.json records that the unpatched image-backed FSDP4/EP4 runtime uses the same ProcessGroup object for FSDP and EP on every rank.
run_artifacts/torchtitan_fsdp_ep_pg_probe_4x_dedicated_ep_20260531.json records that the temporary EP DeviceMesh patch makes FSDP and EP ProcessGroup objects distinct on every rank.
active_proxy_runtime.read: The proxy runtime can be patched for 4x ProcessGroup separation, but the promoted DeepSeek-V4 runtime should be direct TorchTitan and must repeat this probe on the selected image/source tuple.

next_probe.name: fsdp_ep_group_identity_and_overlap_trace
next_probe.when: after patching or swapping the active TorchTitan runtime, or when validating the 8x path
checks: Print fsdp mesh group name, ep mesh group name, rank sets, and object identity from the active image-bundled TorchTitan runtime and require fsdp_group_is_ep_group=false.
Run a 2-step GBS128 trace with RCCL/c10d/rocprof markers and summarize whether FSDP AG/RS and EP AllToAll use different process groups and streams.
Compare exposed EP communication time and FSDP communication time against the Ascend profile categories.

decision.read: The Ascend recipe's highest-transfer AMD lessons are not just sparse kernels. The immediate AMD systems risk was confirmed on the unpatched image-backed 4x runtime and remediated in a patched 4x pg-probe, but the real DeepSeek-V4 canary still needs direct TorchTitan, a materialized DSv4 model/config, and an 8x communication trace. The current DeepSeek-V3 16B proxy canary is useful but does not prove final DSv4-Flash parity.
next_actions_ranked: Materialize torchtitan.models.deepseek_v4 and the DeepSeek-V4 Flash 4-layer BF16 config in the selected TorchTitan checkout.
Run the direct TorchTitan launcher; do not promote the Primus-wrapped DeepSeek-V3 proxy.
Carry the dedicated EP ProcessGroup fix into the selected direct runtime, then rerun the 4x/8x pg-probe.
Preserve current AdamW proxy path; do not switch to Muon or optimizer offload until real model memory or convergence requires it.
Continue single-layer sparse MLA backward fusion around CSA, softmax, and sparse-window families.
Define final DSv4 LightningIndexer and SparseLightningIndexerGradKLLoss contracts before claiming full DeepSeek-V4-Flash coverage.
